medical_history.history_of_present_illness: ___ yo F with a PMH of bronchiectasis, COPD, and persistent pneumonia w/ chronic pseudomonas on tobramycin neb treatment. Pt reports feeling night sweats for the last week. She was last admitted in ___ for Cefepime IV treatment and discharged after PICC placement w/ ~ 2 wks of cefepime. Since then she has been compliant with the pulmonary toilet routine. In ___, she started a 4 weeks on, 4weeks off of tobramycin, but was causing some discomfort, so it was brought down to 2 wk cycles. After the cycles, she reported improvement, but never completely. She has also been taking azithromycin 250mg po daily.
medical_history.past_medical_history:
- COPD on 2L home O2, sat low ___
- Hypercholesterolemia
- Vertigo
- multiple allergies including to yeast, mushrooms, and aspergillus
- Osteoporosis
- Hypertension
- Persistent low weight
- History of cataract extraction
- aspergillus sputum on 1 occasion
- chronic sinusitis
- Pneumonia history: pseudomonas sensitive to quinolones in ___, pseudomonas resistant to quinolones in ___, IV cefepime, and inhaled tobra treatments.
medical_history.family_history: Breast cancer in her mother and prostate cancer in her father. Her daughter and brother have hypertension.

physical_examination.general_examination: Vs: 98.0F, 138/68, 88, 18, 97% 2L nc. General: thin elderly woman in mild respiratory distress.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: HEENT: PERRL, EOMI, dry mucous membranes; Lungs: coarse breath sounds bilaterally; CV: RRR, nl s1, s2, no m/r/g; Abdomen: soft, non-tender, normal bowel sounds; Ext: thin, no edema; Neuro: CN2-12 intact, no focal motor or sensory deficits.
